title: cardamine pratensis subsp. dentata
intro: Cardamine pratensis subsp. dentata, also known as the toothwort, is a flowering plant species within the genus Cardamine, characterized by its unique tooth-like shape on its leaves. Traditionally, toothwort has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of toothwort include its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Toothwort can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in different ways.
